variant: quick heads up: i'm vegetarian and i work out at 6am before work

request.message: quick heads up: i'm vegetarian and i work out at 6am before work

response_body.response: all set. early morning means pre-workout fuel matters—protein + carbs 30-45 min before, nothing too heavy. and any meal plan i give you will stick to vegetarian protein sources: eggs, greek yogurt, tofu, tempeh, lentils, chickpeas, nuts, seeds. what are you working on right now—hairmax, bonemax, or both?

variant: what should i eat after my workout?

request.message: what should i eat after my workout?

response_body.response: post-workout, you want protein + carbs within an hour of finishing. protein drives recovery and muscle retention, carbs refill glycogen and keep your muscles full. for you at 97kg, aim for at least 30g protein per meal. good vegetarian sources: greek yogurt, cottage cheese, eggs, tofu, tempeh, lentils, or a scoop of whey protein powder. pair it with carbs like rice, potatoes, oats, or fruit. simple example: greek yogurt + granola + banana, or scrambled eggs + toast + orange juice. nothing fancy. timing matters more than the exact food.

variant: and when should i eat it?

request.message: and when should i eat it?

response_body.response:
eat it within 60 minutes of finishing your workout. that window is when your muscles are primed to absorb protein and carbs most efficiently. don't overthink the timing—if you're 90 minutes out, still eat it. the sooner the better, but close enough is fine.

since you're working out at 6am, you're probably done by 6:45–7am depending on session length. eat breakfast right after or within the hour. if you're heading straight to work, prep something the night before: overnight oats with protein powder and berries, or a container of greek yogurt with granola. grab it and go.
